narrative_blocks.header: Tuxedo police officer, kennel keeper, and Maine conservationist
narrative_blocks.body: Madeline Hellum Mihok was a charter member of the Tuxedo Volunteer Ambulance Corps, a founder of the Emergency Blood Donor Program, an officer of the Tuxedo Police Department, a nurse at Good Samaritan Hospital in Suffern, New York, and an assistant to her father at the renowned Ledgeland Kennel — then married Louis Gordon Hamersley Jr. in 1975 and moved through Southampton to Sorrento, Maine, where she became an active supporter of the Sorrento Fire Department, Sumner High School, and the Maine State K9 safety vest program. She and Louis shared thirty-three years of marriage and 'a passion for the beauty and serenity of Maine'; she outlived him by fifteen years, dying at age ninety-three in September 2023, a woman of community service who had made careers in medicine, law enforcement, and animal care before spending her final decades as the Hamersley widow of a Maine fishing village.

burial.plot_notes: Ledgelawn Cemetery Bar Harbor Hancock County Maine per FA

narrative_blocks.header: A Manhattan heir who saved the Hudson
narrative_blocks.body: Born to Fifth Avenue and Harvard-educated, he gave his later life to preserving the Hudson River valley — leading Hudson River Heritage and editing the county historical society after a Marine Corps stint and a business career.

sources.label: Edward Hooker, Descendants of Rev. Thomas Hooker (1909)
